sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! Something major happened. The board changed significantly. I can see:
1. A new purple/pink character/object appeared at the bottom left (looks like a little person/robot)
2. The left panel now has gray diamonds at positions that were previously green cells (cols 0, 3)
3. The right panels also have gray diamonds at the corresponding positions
4. The green cells in the left panel are gone (except for the ones in the right panels)
5. The g diamonds and G marker are gone
This is a major state change! Let me examine this more carefully.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Major change detected! Let me examine the new state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("All non-background objects:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] not in ('w', 'W'):
        boundary = n['boundary']
        min_r = min(p[0] for p in boundary)
        min_c = min(p[1] for p in boundary)
        max_r = max(p[0] for p in boundary)
        max_c = max(p[1] for p in boundary)
        print(f"  {n['color']}: pixels={n['pixels']}, top-left=({min_r},{min_c}), size=({max_r-min_r+1}x{max_c-min_c+1})")

# Also check what the character looks like
print("\nNew character area (bottom-left):")
lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
for i in range(48, min(62, len(lines))):
    print(f"  Row {i}: {lines[i][:15]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Wow! Clicking on the g diamond triggered a major transformation. A character (purple/pink robot-like figure) appeared at the bottom left. The board now has:
- g diamonds at 4 positions: (18,11), (18,35), (24,41), (36,41)
- A purple character at the bottom left (rows 51-61, cols 2-10)
- Black borders and blue borders
The g diamonds are now at:
1. (18, 11) - left panel
2. (18, 35) - left panel  
3. (24, 41) - right panel 1
4. (36, 41) - right panel 2
So clicking the g diamond "activated" the level and spawned a character. Now the game is probably about navigating this character. Let me check if the directional keys now work with this character.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content: World model: BREAKTHROUGH! Clicking the g diamond after it appears spawns a purple character at bottom-left. The g diamonds are now at 4 positions across both panels. The game has transitioned to a navigation phase with the character.

        min_c = min(p[1] for p in boundary)
        max_r = max(p[0] for p in boundary)
        max_c = max(p[1] for p in boundary)
        print(f"Character: top-left=({min_r},{min_c}), size=({max_r-min_r+1}x{max_c-min_c+1})")
